articles.title: Why People Are Upset About a Singer at a Music Festival
articles.summary: A music festival in London called Wireless Festival has booked a famous singer named Kanye West, also known as Ye, to perform in July. Many people are angry about this because Kanye has said and done things that hurt Jewish people. For example, he made a song with a bad name and sold shirts with a hateful symbol. Jewish groups and politicians want the festival to cancel his show and stop him from coming to the UK. They say his actions are wrong and make Jewish people feel unsafe. Some companies that were helping with the festival, like Pepsi and PayPal, have stopped supporting it because of this. The festival has rules against discrimination, but it still booked Kanye, which has caused a big argument. People are asking the government to step in and not let Kanye into the country.

articles.title: Jessica Pegula Wins Tennis Tournament Again
articles.summary: Jessica Pegula is a tennis player from America who won a big tournament called the Charleston Open. She played against Yuliia Starodubtseva, who was in her first final, and won easily with scores of 6-2 and 6-2. This is Pegula's second win this year after she also won in Dubai. She had to play many tough matches that went to three sets to reach the final, and she saved many break points in this match. Pegula thanked the fans for supporting her all week. Starodubtseva made many mistakes but moved up in the world rankings. Pegula now has more wins this year than the top player Aryna Sabalenka.

articles.title: NHS Workers Sing in a Play About Germs
articles.summary: A fun musical play in London is about antibiotic resistance, which happens when germs stop responding to medicines. It stars 60 NHS healthcare workers like doctors and nurses who sing and act. The play tells the story of Sir Alexander Fleming, who discovered penicillin in 1928, and a junior doctor today. It shows how important it is to use antibiotics carefully so they keep working. The performers say singing makes them happy and gives them energy, even after long hospital shifts. The play has been performed in Edinburgh and at the United Nations to teach people about this big problem. It helps everyone understand why we need to protect our medicines from superbugs.

articles.title: A New Kind of Battery That Charges Faster When It Gets Bigger
articles.summary: Scientists in Australia have made a special battery called a quantum battery. It uses tiny particles and light to store energy, which is different from regular batteries that use chemicals. This new battery can charge, hold, and release energy. The cool part is that it charges faster as it gets bigger, unlike normal batteries that don't change speed with size. The scientists built a small model to show how it works, and they hope to make bigger ones in the future. This could help store more energy for things like phones or cars, making them charge quicker and last longer. The research was done by a team from CSIRO, RMIT University, and the University of Melbourne, and they shared their findings in a science journal.

articles.title: Comedians Ask for Help to Keep Comedy Fun
articles.summary: Comedians in Britain are asking the government for more money to help new comedians. Comedy is very popular, but it does not get much support from the state. Big comedians sell out arenas, but small places where young comedians learn need help. Comedy leaders met with ministers to say comedy should be seen as an art form like music or theatre. This could help get more funding. The Culture Minister wants to work with comedians to find ways to help. Some comedians say comedy is risky, and clubs need money to try new acts. They believe comedy needs diverse voices to stay interesting. Without support, the next generation of comedians might not succeed.

articles.title: Jamie and Sophie's Family Show
articles.summary: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo are a couple who met on a TV show called Made in Chelsea. People have watched their relationship grow from dating to getting married. Now, they are having a baby and are sharing this special time in a new documentary called Raising Chelsea. The show follows them as they try to have a baby, go through pregnancy, and become parents. They film everything, even the birth, because they want to be real and honest with their fans. They use only one camera to make it feel like a home video, so they act naturally. Jamie says they show all moments, good and bad, because that's what real life is like.
